shortMeaningInSentence: to distribute resources for a purpose
exampleEn: The government decided to allocate more funds to public education this year.
exampleZh: 政府决定今年 allocate 更多资金给公共教育

shortMeaningInSentence: open to more than one interpretation
exampleEn: The results of the experiment were ambiguous and required further analysis.
exampleZh: 实验结果很 ambiguous 需要进一步分析

shortMeaningInSentence: a raw material that can be bought and sold
exampleEn: Oil is one of the most traded commodities in the global market.
exampleZh: 石油是全球市场上交易量最大的 commodity 之一

shortMeaningInSentence: prolonged public disagreement
exampleEn: The new policy caused a great deal of controversy among the public.
exampleZh: 新政策在公众中引起了很大的 controversy

shortMeaningInSentence: to make or become less
exampleEn: The pain will gradually diminish over the next few hours.
exampleZh: 疼痛会在接下来的几小时内逐渐 diminish

shortMeaningInSentence: equal in value or meaning
exampleEn: One mile is roughly equivalent to 1.6 kilometers.
exampleZh: 一英里 roughly equivalent to 1.6 公里

shortMeaningInSentence: to rise and fall irregularly
exampleEn: Stock prices tend to fluctuate throughout the trading day.
exampleZh: 股票价格在交易日中往往会 fluctuate

shortMeaningInSentence: a proposed explanation for a phenomenon
exampleEn: The scientist tested her hypothesis through a series of experiments.
exampleZh: 科学家通过一系列实验验证了她的 hypothesis

shortMeaningInSentence: to put a plan into action
exampleEn: The company plans to implement the new security system next month.
exampleZh: 公司计划下个月 implement 新的安全系统

shortMeaningInSentence: certain to happen and unavoidable
exampleEn: Change is inevitable in any growing organization.
exampleZh: 在任何成长中的组织里 变化都是 inevitable 的

shortMeaningInSentence: to explain the meaning of something
exampleEn: We need to interpret the data before drawing any conclusions.
exampleZh: 在得出结论之前 我们需要 interpret 这些数据

shortMeaningInSentence: movement from one place to another
exampleEn: The annual migration of birds is a remarkable natural phenomenon.
exampleZh: 鸟类每年的 migration 是一个了不起的自然现象

shortMeaningInSentence: a fact or event that can be observed
exampleEn: The northern lights are a beautiful natural phenomenon.
exampleZh: 北极光是一种美丽的自然 phenomenon

shortMeaningInSentence: a part or share of a whole
exampleEn: A large proportion of students find part-time jobs during their studies.
exampleZh: 很大 proportion 的学生在学习期间做兼职

shortMeaningInSentence: closely connected to the matter at hand
exampleEn: Please provide only the information that is relevant to this case.
exampleZh: 请只提供与本案 relevant 的信息

shortMeaningInSentence: complex and refined
exampleEn: The laboratory is equipped with sophisticated instruments for research.
exampleZh: 实验室配备了 sophisticated 的研究仪器

shortMeaningInSentence: able to be maintained at a certain rate
exampleEn: We need to find sustainable solutions for our energy needs.
exampleZh: 我们需要为能源需求找到 sustainable 的解决方案

shortMeaningInSentence: to experience or be subjected to
exampleEn: All new employees must undergo a training session before starting work.
exampleZh: 所有新员工必须在开始工作前 undergo 培训

shortMeaningInSentence: logically sound and acceptable
exampleEn: You need a valid passport to travel abroad.
exampleZh: 你需要一本 valid 护照才能出国旅行

shortMeaningInSentence: the health and happiness of a person or group
exampleEn: The organization works to improve the welfare of children in need.
exampleZh: 该组织致力于改善贫困儿童的 welfare
